description_long: This shotgun features a boxy, utilitarian design with a rectangular metal receiver and a short, cylindrical barrel protruding from the front. The finish is a matte, dark gray metal with visible wear and subtle surface texture, suggesting heavy use or industrial manufacture. The magazine is a straight, rectangular protrusion beneath the receiver, and a simple trigger and trigger guard are visible. The top of the receiver has a small cylindrical sight post and a rear sight notch, while the muzzle end displays a perforated barrel shroud with three vent holes. The grip is integrated into the lower receiver, appearing as a straight, vertical handle without a traditional stock. The overall form is compact and functional, with minimal decorative elements.
description: A compact, boxy shotgun with a matte gray metal finish, straight magazine, and integrated vertical grip. The design is utilitarian, with a perforated barrel shroud and minimal external features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular barrel opening, perforated shroud, and the narrow, rectangular profile of the receiver.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the receiver, the straight magazine, trigger, and integrated vertical grip.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the flat back of the receiver and the alignment of the barrel and sights.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the magazine, trigger, and barrel shroud from the other side.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, short with a perforated shroud at the muzzle
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rectangular, elongated trigger guard with straight edges and no visible finger grooves
